item.title: Plooms of dust on road, 29th April
item.description:
Dirt, stones and silt debris is being washed from the hill and carpark onto the road outside my house affecting my neighbours respectively. In winter when it rains I have to dig channels to allow the dirt and water to run past the frontages and into the neighbouring field to prevent flooding of driveway and garage and when it's dry from spring to end of glsummer the dust caused from the muck being disturbed by walkers and cars as they pass by leaving clouds of dust which stay hanging in the air for a long time then settle all over the cars, windows and house as a whole. The dust disturbance is totally stopping my family from being able to enjoy using the front of the garden which has seating. Any time spent outside the front of the house causes my wife's asthma to flare up and my son is complaining of a sore throat. The back garden is now being affected by the dust as it travels in the breeze so we are really concerned we won't be able to use our back garden if this muck is not regularlary cleaned away. We have lived here two years and we are surprised that this hasnt been reviewed by the council owing to the disturbance this is causing not to mention learning that a road sweeper did indeed used to be employed here to tackle this issue but has since fallen by the wayside. We would really appreciate a review of this issue and I am happy to meet with you and arrange with my neighbours who are also affected by this to come to some resolution as this simply cannot continue. We feel this would have been picked up if it were in a suburban street but due to our location we feel we are not being taken seriously as pot holes are constantly being reported but very few are being resolved. Thank you for taking the time to read this and I look forward to your response. Kind regards James

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Green Street, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Other problem, 29th April
item.description:
